top_categories.workout_context: La Stelvio Santini Granfondo's mountain passes forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 94 miles.
160ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Race-pace efforts that mimic La Stelvio Santini Granfondo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
At La Stelvio Santini Granfondo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
15,010ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
15,010ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of La Stelvio Santini Granfondo. Train it until it feels boring.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At La Stelvio Santini Granfondo, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
At mile 60 of 94, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mountain passes. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Low-cadence force work for 15,010ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 94 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for La Stelvio Santini Granfondo’s 94 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at La Stelvio Santini Granfondo. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Highly technical terrain at La Stelvio Santini Granfondo demands practice on similar surfaces. Ride mountain passes at race-day cadence weekly. Practice cornering, descending, and power delivery on unstable surfaces. Dial in tire pressure before race week — 5 PSI wrong costs you 15+ minutes over 94 miles.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Gravel Specific, TT Threshold, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 94 miles of mountain passes in Bormio, Alta Valtellina, Lombardy, Italy.
